title: centaurea pallescens subsp. calcitrapella
intro: Centaurea pallescens subsp. calcitrapella, also known as the Cornish knapweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family. It is native to the western parts of Europe and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a treatment for various gastrointestinal issues, including diarrhea and dysentery. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. The Cornish knapweed can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to alleviate digestive issues and promote overall well-being.
